questions.question: What is the primary function of a D flip-flop in digital circuits?
options: To store a single bit of data and update it synchronously on a clock edge.
To perform arithmetic operations like addition or subtraction.
To combine multiple input signals into a single output.
To divide the input clock frequency by two.
To amplify a weak digital signal.

questions.question: In the context of CPU caches, what does "N-way set associative" imply about how cache lines are mapped from main memory?
options: Each block from main memory can be placed in any location within the cache.
Each block from main memory can be placed in exactly one specific location in the cache.
Each block from main memory can be placed in any of N specific locations (ways) within a particular set in the cache.
The cache is organized into N independent banks, each handling a different memory address range.
N refers to the number of levels in the cache hierarchy (e.g., L1, L2, L3).

questions.question: What is the primary objective of High-Level Synthesis (HLS) in the digital IC design flow?
options: To verify the correctness of a gate-level netlist against its RTL description.
To automatically generate Register-Transfer Level (RTL) code from an algorithmic description written in a high-level language like C, C++, or SystemC.
To optimize the physical placement of standard cells on the silicon die.
To perform detailed timing analysis after physical layout is complete.
To characterize the power consumption of individual standard cells.

questions.question: In high-performance multi-master bus architectures (e.g., AMBA AXI), what is the key advantage of a "split-transaction" bus protocol compared to a "single-transaction" bus?
options: It simplifies the arbitration logic by allowing only one master to access the bus at a time.
It reduces the total number of wires required for the bus interface.
It allows the bus to be released by a master after issuing a request, enabling other masters to use the bus while the requested data is being fetched, thereby improving bus utilization.
It guarantees cache coherence across all masters without additional hardware.
It enables simultaneous read and write operations on the same address.

questions.question: In Design for Testability (DFT) for digital ICs, what is the primary purpose of converting sequential elements (like flip-flops) into scan chains?
options: To reduce the dynamic power consumption of the chip during normal operation.
To facilitate easier observation and control of internal node states, enabling high fault coverage for manufacturing defects through automated test pattern generation (ATPG).
To improve the clock frequency and overall performance of the circuit.
To decrease the silicon area required for the design.
To implement built-in self-test (BIST) capabilities without requiring external test equipment.
